title: Confirm Your Calling and Election
subtitle: Partakers of divine nature, adding virtue to faith, and the prophetic word confirmed

heb.paragraph: A faith of equal standing with ours - the recipients share the same precious faith as the apostles. No second-class Christians.
Partakers of the divine nature - a striking phrase. Believers share in God nature through the promises, escaping corruption.
Knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord - epignosis is intensive knowledge, not mere information but transformative acquaintance.

panels.ctx: Peter writes to those who have obtained a faith of equal standing with the apostles through the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ. Grace and peace are multiplied through knowledge of God and Jesus. His divine power has granted all things for life and godliness through knowledge of him who called us. He has granted precious and very great promises so that through them we may become partakers of the divine nature, having escaped the corruption in the world caused by sinful desire.

cross.note: Peter greeting to elect exiles - parallel salutation.
This is eternal life, that they know you, the only true God.
Let us cleanse ourselves from every defilement of body and spirit.
In him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily, and you have been filled in him.

notes.note: Simeon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ - both titles emphasize authority and humility.
Grace and peace multiplied through knowledge. Divine power grants all for life and godliness. Precious promises make us partakers of divine nature.
Calvin notes that partakers of divine nature does not mean we become gods but that we share in communicable attributes - holiness, love, knowledge - by grace.
The phrase our God and Savior Jesus Christ applies both titles to Jesus - a clear affirmation of deity.
Divine nature (theias phuseos) echoes Hellenistic philosophical language but is transformed by biblical monotheism.
Schreiner emphasizes that the promises are the means of participation in divine nature. Transformation comes through trusting God word.
Davids notes that escaping corruption is not Platonic flight from matter but moral transformation - freedom from sin desire.

heb.paragraph: Supplement your faith with virtue - arete is moral excellence, the habit of right action. Faith is the foundation; virtue is the first addition.
Make every effort - the call is to diligent pursuit. Grace does not eliminate effort but empowers it.
An entrance into the eternal kingdom will be richly provided - the metaphor is triumphal entry, lavish welcome.

panels.ctx: For this very reason, make every effort to supplement faith with virtue, virtue with knowledge, knowledge with self-control, self-control with steadfastness, steadfastness with godliness, godliness with brotherly affection, brotherly affection with love. If these qualities are yours and increasing, they keep you from being ineffective or unfruitful. Whoever lacks them is blind and has forgotten cleansing from former sins. Therefore be all the more diligent to confirm your calling and election - those who practice these things will never fall. An entrance into the eternal kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ will be richly provided.

cross.note: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace - similar virtue list.
I press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call.
Show the same earnestness to have the full assurance of hope.
Whoever hates his brother is in darkness and walks in darkness.

notes.note: Make every effort: faith, virtue, knowledge, self-control, steadfastness, godliness, brotherly affection, love - a chain of growth.
These qualities increasing keep you effective and fruitful. Lacking them means blindness and forgotten cleansing.
Be diligent to confirm your calling and election. Practice these things and you will never fall. Rich entrance into the eternal kingdom.
Calvin sees the virtues as a ladder - each builds on the previous. Faith is the root; love is the crown. The sequence is not arbitrary.
Confirmation of election is not earning it but evidencing it. Works do not cause election but confirm it.
The verb supplement (epichoregeo) means to supply lavishly - the same word used of wealthy patrons funding theatrical productions.
Forgotten cleansing - perhaps baptismal forgetfulness. The person has lost sight of the significance of conversion.
Schreiner emphasizes that the virtue ladder is not merit-earning but assurance-building. Growing Christians are confident Christians.
Davids notes that the list moves from individual virtues (self-control) to communal (brotherly affection) to universal (love). Faith matures into love.

heb.paragraph: After my departure - the term exodus evokes the great deliverance. Peter sees his death as a departure to glory.
Eyewitnesses of his majesty - Peter was present at the Transfiguration. The testimony is firsthand, not secondhand myth.
Men spoke from God as they were carried along by the Holy Spirit - inspiration is divine initiative. The Spirit moves; prophets speak.

panels.ctx: Peter intends to remind them of these things though they know them. He knows his departure is soon - as the Lord Jesus made clear to him. He will make every effort so that after his departure they can recall these things. For they did not follow cleverly devised myths when they made known the power and coming of the Lord Jesus Christ, but were eyewitnesses of his majesty at the Transfiguration. The voice from heaven confirmed: This is my beloved Son. We have the prophetic word more fully confirmed - a lamp shining in a dark place until the day dawns and the morning star rises. No prophecy of Scripture comes from someone own interpretation - men spoke from God as they were carried along by the Holy Spirit.

cross.note: Jesus predicting Peter death - the prophecy Peter references.
The Transfiguration account - Peter was there.
Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.
All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching.

notes.note: Peter will remind them always. His departure (exodus) is soon, as the Lord showed him. He wants them to recall these things after he is gone.
Not cleverly devised myths but eyewitness testimony. At the Transfiguration they saw his majesty and heard the voice from heaven.
The prophetic word is more fully confirmed - a lamp in a dark place. No prophecy comes from human interpretation but from the Spirit carrying men along.
Calvin contrasts apostolic testimony with pagan mythology. The gospel is not fable but fact, attested by eyewitnesses.
Scripture interpretation requires the same Spirit who inspired. Human ingenuity alone cannot unlock divine meaning.
The term exodus for death is unusual - used of Jesus death in Luke 9:31 at the Transfiguration, linking Peter death to Christ.
Carried along (pheromenoi) is a nautical term - like a ship driven by wind. The Spirit provides the propulsion; prophets are the vessels.
Schreiner emphasizes that vv. 19-21 are foundational for the doctrine of inspiration. Scripture is divine speech through human authors.
Davids notes that Peter appeal to eyewitness testimony anticipates the false teachers who will claim superior knowledge. Apostolic witness trumps speculation.

rows.label: vv. 5-11
rows.text: Virtue ladder; confirm calling and election

lit.note: Chapter 1 establishes foundations: divine promises (vv. 1-4), moral growth (vv. 5-11), and Scripture authority (vv. 12-21). Peter prepares for his departure.

themes.note: Faith and holiness dominate the virtue ladder. Prophecy is strong in the inspiration passage. Sovereignty appears in divine calling and election.
